a.xlink:title: The Ability Hypothesis — experience teaches know-how, so Mary learns no new fact
The Knowledge Argument — Mary learns a fact the physical description omits
What Mary acquires on first seeing red is a set of abilities, not knowledge of a new fact
The Knowledge Argument (Mary the colour scientist)
The access asymmetry is unavailable because first-person access is reciprocally non-transferable
First-person access to my own suffering grounds a moral-status asymmetry
The first-person/third-person access gap is symmetric across any two subjects, never singling out machines
First-person access yields a warranted moral-status asymmetry favouring oneself over a machine
First-person access grounds no moral-status asymmetry that singles out machines
First-person acquaintance with a subject's experience is necessary for justified attribution of moral status to it
First-person access (privileged access)
The Acquaintance Hypothesis — Mary meets the experience rather than learning a truth
What Mary acquires on first seeing red is acquaintance with the experience, not knowledge of a new fact
The actual–possible seam — perceptual streams are actual, action options are possibilia, and a duality should not straddle that line
Free will is the categorical dual of consciousness, coproduct-like selection mirroring product-like integration
Categorical duality (arrow reversal, products and coproducts)
The mirror duality between the consciousness and free-will debates
The conceivability-certificate objection transferred — libertarian agent-causation's possibility cannot be cogently asserted a priori
The ideal conceivability of libertarian agent-causation is a consistency claim no computable reasoner can certify
Dialectical cogency requires premises with accessible, non-question-begging justification
Reliably certifying the consistency of arithmetic-rich scenarios is at least as hard as the halting problem
A scenario's ideal conceivability is its consistency under ideal a priori reasoning
Agent causation
The halting oracle (and uncomputability)
Self-model architecture predicts an advanced LLM will exhibit its own hard-problem form
A sufficiently advanced LLM could exhibit its own version of the hard problem — a raw feel, under its own concept of consciousness, that mechanistic self-knowledge cannot explain from inside
No finite system can losslessly represent its entire current state within itself
An LLM's introspective access reaches the similarity structure of its internal states but not the encoding that carries it
Some current LLMs exhibit limited, unreliable functional introspective awareness of their internal states
Access consciousness vs. phenomenal consciousness
The hard problem of consciousness
Varieties of introspective explanatory gap (ontological / architectural-access / conceptual-translation / training-contamination)
First-order vs higher-order mental states
The collapse problem — consciousness is the ambient medium of both poles, not one pole of the duality
The anti-zombie argument — an anti-zombie is as conceivable as a zombie, so the modal bridge proves too much
A microphysical duplicate of us whose physical nature necessitates consciousness is conceivable
Ideal conceivability entails metaphysical possibility
Type-A / Type-B / Type-C materialism (responses to the hard problem)
Philosophical zombie
If LLMs have human-like attributes, then so does Age of Empires II
Assuming anthropomorphic attributes a priori, independent of substrate, yields circular or uninformative conclusions
The anthropomorphic attributes ascribed to LLMs are empirically non-unique across substrates
A trainable neural network can be realized in any sufficiently powerful substrate
Age of Empires II circuits (a trained perceptron built in a videogame)
Chinese Nation (homunculi-headed robot)
Functionalism (about mental states)
The architectural account explains an access gap, not the phenomenal one
Removing a system's access barriers to self-explanation need not remove the phenomenal explanatory gap
A system can be fully mechanistically explicable from outside yet unable to derive a satisfying explanation of its own introspective appearances from inside
Behavioural parity is evidence of mental status
Some current LLMs realise some mental states
Mental states are substrate-independent
The bike rider learns facts — the ability hypothesis rests on a false dichotomy
Knowing how to do something is a species of propositional knowledge
The blockhead dilemma — physically impossible, or imaginatively under-determined
The lookup-table (blockhead) objection — behaviour is not sufficient for mind
For a merely conceivable lookup table, the intuition that it lacks a mind is not compelled
A system that produces behaviour solely by stored lookup has no mental states
A lookup table reproducing a human- or LLM-scale behavioural profile is physically unrealisable
Lookup-table mind ("Blockhead" / Aunt Bubbles machine)
Block's bounded-table rejoinder — a lifetime-bounded table escapes the combinatorial reply
The combinatorial-impossibility reply — a finite lookup table cannot match an open-ended mind's behaviour
A finite mind also fails on inputs beyond its own capacity
A lookup table can be bounded to cover every input a subject encounters in a lifetime
No finite lookup table can reproduce the full behavioural profile of a genuine mind
A lookup table can reproduce a mind's behavioural profile without any internal causal organisation
The Brain Simulator Reply — simulate the neuron firings of a real Chinese speaker
Executing a formal program is not sufficient for understanding
The Chinese Room
The causal argument for physicalism
Some mental events cause physical effects
Mental events are physical events
The physical effects of mental causes are not systematically overdetermined
Every physical effect has a sufficient physical cause
Causal closure of the physical
Completeness of physics
Kim's causal exclusion (supervenience) argument
A mental property that supervenes on but is not identical to its physical base is causally excluded by that base
The causal-structure reply — objective counterfactual structure supplies the invariant
The decomposition-indeterminacy objection — determinate consciousness cannot rest on observer-relative function
Objective causal structure singles out a privileged functional decomposition
Counterfactual-causal dependencies are objective, not observer-relative
A physical system has no unique functional decomposition
Implementing a computation requires the right counterfactual dispositions across the whole transition table
The mind-individuating functional role is fixed at the grain of internal counterfactual-causal organisation
Grain (of role individuation)
The Chinese Nation realises the organisation yet lacks qualia — organisation is not sufficient for consciousness
A population realising a mind's functional organisation would nonetheless have no phenomenal consciousness
A population can realise a mind's full functional-causal organisation, not merely its input-output behaviour
Phenomenally conscious states are not exhausted by functional/causal role
The Chinese Room — running a program is not sufficient for understanding
The person executing the Chinese Room program understands no Chinese
A person who understands no Chinese can, by executing a formal program, produce responses indistinguishable from a Chinese speaker's
The Chinese Room as a derivation from axioms — syntax is not sufficient for minds
Brains cause minds through their specific biological causal powers
Minds have semantic content (genuine meaning), not merely syntax
Computer programs are purely formal (syntactic)
Implementing a program is neither constitutive of nor sufficient for having a mind
Intentionality (aboutness)
Strong AI vs. weak AI
McGinn's cognitive-closure argument for mysterianism
A type of mind can be constitutively incapable of grasping some concepts
There is a natural property of the brain that explains consciousness
Humans are cognitively closed to the property that explains how the brain produces consciousness
The combination problem — micro-subjects cannot compose a macro-subject
Consciousness is fundamental, present even in the simplest physical entities
Distinct micro-subjects cannot sum into a further macro-subject
Conceivability is not a reliable guide to possibility (the a-posteriori-necessity objection)
Some necessary truths can be known only a posteriori
The halting oracle shows conceivability does not entail physical realizability
Conceivability does not entail physical realizability
The halting oracle is mathematically coherent yet physically unrealizable
The Connectionist Reply — a massively parallel, brain-like architecture is the real candidate for understanding
The conscious-USA counter-analogy — high integration without intuitive subjecthood breaks the scale-free inference
Subjects can combine — observed merging and a graded subject-count answer the combination problem
The argument from mirrored dialectics — arrow-reversal maps the consciousness debate onto the free-will debate
The options of a choice are mutually exclusive, structured as a coproduct of which exactly one summand is realized
The categorical dual of volition is attention, input-side selection mirroring output-side selection
The combination problem for panpsychism dualizes to the composition problem for agency
The contents of a conscious moment are co-present, structured as a conjunctive product of qualia channels
The free-will debate is the consciousness debate mirror-imaged along the perception–action axis
Pereboom's disappearing-agent objection is the arrow-reversed image of Kim's causal-exclusion argument
The problem of the unity of agency is the arrow-reversed image of the problem of the unity of consciousness
The philosophical zombie dualizes to the volitional zombie, a behaviorally identical agent with no real choosing
A consciousness-corpus-trained model's hard-problem testimony carries zero evidential weight, not reduced weight
Counterfactuals buy non-triviality, not uniqueness
Counterfactual constraints yield non-triviality but not a unique decomposition
Dancing qualia — a functional isomorph with inverted qualia would have its experience flip undetectably, which is absurd
Systems with the same fine-grained functional organization have qualitatively identical experiences
The dancing/fading-qualia reductio begs the question by presupposing functionalism about introspection
The explanatory gap is predicted by strong functionalism (phenomenal concepts + dancing-qualia)
The dancing/fading-qualia reductio derives its contradiction only by presupposing functionalism about introspective judgement
Continuity–connectedness argument schema (no-sharp-line / sorites form)
The dance leaves no memory trace — "noticing" a qualia flip needs a cross-time comparison whose earlier term is functionally fixed
The paradox of phenomenal judgment
De novo human origination breaks the report-scepticism parity — the contamination rule's trigger is met for AI and unmet for the human originators
Report-scepticism proves too much — applied uniformly, its discount rules screen off human hard-problem reports as well
Humans originated the hard-problem concept de novo, with no prior template to copy
Functionalism owes an observer-independent invariant that fixes a system's decomposition
There is a determinate fact about one's own current phenomenal state
The dense China-brain regress reopens the grain dilemma against the organisational middle
The organisational grain is a principled middle that escapes both horns of the grain dilemma
The organisational-role grain either readmits the blockhead or smuggles sub-organisational features back in
The bad-explanation argument against solipsism — it reconstructs realism and adds only surplus
A mind-independent external world exists
A good explanation is one that is hard to vary while still accounting for what it explains
Solipsism must reconstruct the external world within the self, so it amounts to realism plus unexplained surplus
Hinge proposition
Pereboom's disappearing-agent objection to event-causal libertarianism
In an undetermined event-causal decision, nothing about the agent settles which option occurs
The dispositionalist reply: meaning is fixed by idealized dispositions
Kripke's Wittgenstein: the skeptical argument about rule-following and meaning
A speaker's meaning is constituted by their dispositions to use the expression
There is no fact about a speaker that constitutes their meaning one thing rather than another by an expression
If causal exclusion is sound, causal powers drain away from every level above fundamental physics
Higher-level mental causes can be genuine difference-making causes even though every physical effect has a sufficient physical cause
The explanatory gap is evidence against uniform substrate-independence
Fading qualia — a part-silicon functional isomorph cannot have faded experience without being radically wrong about it
The "real feel" is first-person observed, not unobservable — the idleness charge presupposes that observation is third-person observation
The "really feels vs merely reports" residue is unobservable in principle — judged by coherence and usefulness, the relativized claim stands and the residue is idle
Formulating the hard problem from the first-person side requires access to a representation of one's own phenomenal-seeming states
Each subject has direct, non-inferential first-person acquaintance with its own conscious states
A finite system cannot completely represent its own current state (a capacity bound)
A finite system can be given complete internal access to its own current state
A system's internal vantage on itself is informationally poorer than an external vantage
A finite system's self-representation is part of the very state it represents
One has no first-person acquaintance with a machine's putative experience
Fission — psychological continuity can hold one-to-many, so it cannot be the identity relation
A person at one time is identical to a person at another just in case they are linked by overlapping chains of psychological connection
Personal identity is not what matters in survival
The genetic argument — physicalism plus realism about experience entails panpsychism
Experience cannot emerge brutely from the wholly non-experiential
Phenomenal consciousness is genuinely real, not an illusion
The graded-structure objection — attention makes experience partly exclusive, action is partly conjunctive, so the crude product/coproduct assignment idealizes both sides
The halting oracle is coherent yet physically unrealizable
No Turing machine decides the halting problem
Every physically realizable computation is Turing-computable
The Indexical Reply — Mary learns where she is in the world, not what is in the world
What Mary acquires on first seeing red is self-locating knowledge of how she herself reacts to red, not a new general fact
The intrinsic-nature argument — matter's hidden categorical nature is experiential
Consciousness is an intrinsic property of whatever has it
Consciousness is the only intrinsic nature we are directly acquainted with
A functional description captures only structural-dynamical facts, not intrinsic nature
A thing's relational-structural properties must be grounded in its intrinsic nature
Intrinsic vs. relational (structural) properties
Confabulation studies show introspective reports are theory-driven constructions
Introspective access to one's experience is itself a fallible, constructed representation
A self-explanatory gap follows from limited internal access plus non-traversable self-explanation
An explanation is intuitively satisfying for a system only if it is traversable by that system's own cognitive operations
The intuition-domain asymmetry — the cosmic table is a far extrapolation, the brain-scale isomorph a near neighbour
Deflating the cosmic-table intuition does not deflate intuitions about brain-scale duplicates
An intuition's evidential weight tracks the case's resemblance to the exemplars it was formed on
Phenomenal consciousness is a fundamental, non-physical feature of reality
Before leaving the room, Mary knows all the physical facts about colour vision
On first seeing red, Mary learns something she did not know before
There are truths about conscious experience not deducible from the complete physical description
Intrinsic vs. derived (observer-relative) intentionality
The LLM-representations reply — actual text-trained models build human-aligned representations, pressing A3 empirically
Language models trained on text alone develop internal representations structurally aligned with human ones
Language models have a functional global workspace because their verbalizable representations bear the five-property signature of conscious access
Verbal reportability, top-down modulation, mediation of deliberate reasoning, flexible generalization, and selectivity jointly constitute the functional signature of conscious access
The contents of a language model's J-space are subject to top-down control
A J-space vector lifted from one context is correctly operated on by whatever function a new context supplies
A language model's J-space carries the unspoken intermediate steps of its multi-step reasoning
A language model's J-space is required only for the flexible, non-automatic fraction of its processing
A language model, asked what it is thinking about, names the concepts represented in its J-space
Language models maintain a privileged set of verbalizable representations that plays the functional role of a global workspace
The J-space — the subspace of verbalizable representations in a language model
LLMary — a model that has read all of AI but never seen token 123 updates on first encounter, yet the whole episode is physical, refuting the Knowledge Argument's conditional
LLMary (the language model that never saw token 123)
For any finite lookup table there are finite inputs outside its stored domain, to which it can only "respond" by ignoring the excess of the input
A genuine mind can respond appropriately to novel inputs beyond any fixed finite stock
Matching the full behavioural profile of a mental state is not sufficient for having that state
The cosmic-size reply — a behaviour-matching lookup table is too large to physically exist
A lookup table reproducing behaviour over a context of n tokens from a vocabulary of v requires on the order of v^n entries
The observable universe can store only a bounded, finite amount of information (on the order of 10^120 bits)
The Luminous Room — Searle's anti-computation intuition is as unreliable as a 19th-century intuition that magnets can't make light
The Many Mansions Reply — even if programs fail, some other artificial means could yield a mind
The equivocation reply — reading Mary's gain as a new fact rather than a new acquaintance begs the question against physicalism
The Mary intuition misimagines the premise — "all physical facts" is quietly read as "lots of facts"
Mary, genuinely knowing all the physical facts, would learn nothing on first seeing red
Mary's complete physical knowledge is unrealizable — an embedded knower cannot encode the total state that contains her
The scope reply — Mary's case bounds only a-priori/deducibility physicalism, leaving a-posteriori physicalism standing
Modal rationalism — ideal conceivability is a guide to possibility
Kripkean a-posteriori necessities are misdescribed genuine possibilities, not conceived impossibilities
Primary intension
Secondary intension
The circularity objection — Moore's proof begs the question against the skeptic
Moore's proof of an external world — here is a hand
The existence of the external world cannot be demonstrated without circularity
Dialectical cogency (probative force)
The regress argument — complete justification must regress, circle, or break off
Every attempt at complete justification ends in regress, circularity, or a dogmatic stopping point
The Münchhausen trilemma (Agrippa's regress)
For occurrent experience the appearance is the reality, so introspection of it cannot misrepresent wholesale
Introspective acquaintance with one's current experience is a direct datum, not a fallible representation of it
The continuity argument — no non-arbitrary line where experience switches on
There is no non-arbitrary point in nature at which experience first appears
The missing universal property — without one, the duality licenses no theorem transfer
Arrow-reversal from the consciousness domain to the free-will domain yields a heuristic dictionary, not a transfer theorem
Old fact, new guise — Mary's new knowledge is a new phenomenal concept, not a new fact
What Mary acquires on first seeing red is a new phenomenal concept of a physical fact she already knew
Maudlin's Olympia argument against computationalism about consciousness
The quantum counterfactuals objection — unexercised possibilities have physical effects, and quantum runs have no trace to unroll
The Klara→Olympia transformation is trace specialization with guarded deoptimization
The recording objection — every Olympia descends from a genuine run of the program, so the trilemma's horns lose their absurdity
The grain dilemma for functional "role" (too liberal vs. too chauvinistic)
The Other Minds Reply — we attribute understanding to others on behavioural grounds, so deny it to the room only by a double standard
Gradual psychological rewiring (Parfit's spectrum) forces identity to admit of degree
Personal identity is not all-or-nothing but admits of degree
Altering a single psychological connection never changes who someone is
The phenomenal-concept strategy over-generates — it debunks the self-knowledge it presupposes
A phenomenal concept cannot be at once informative enough to ground self-knowledge while isolated enough to deflate the explanatory gap
The self-model architecture's prediction is phenomenality-neutral — it forecasts hard-problem reports, which an experience-free system would emit too
For a concept positing differences unobservable in principle, the only available standards of assessment are coherence plus usefulness
Attribution vs. detection — how we know a mind is not what a mind is
The Chinese Gym — a parallel crowd of clerks understands no more than one
Internalise the rulebook — the person becomes the whole system and still understands nothing
The Systems Reply — the whole system understands, though the person does not
Room in the robot — the man manipulates the robot's symbols and still attaches no meaning
The Robot Reply — symbols grounded in sensorimotor commerce acquire meaning
Many Mansions trivialises strong AI — the reply concedes the thesis it was meant to save
The water-pipe brain — simulating the form of neural firing reproduces structure, not causal power
The relevance-filter regress — selecting mind-relevant causal relations re-raises the invariant demand
The physical facts about a system include every causal disposition, not only the cognitively relevant ones
The retorsion argument — asserting external-world skepticism presupposes its own falsity
One can know a proposition without being able to demonstrate it non-circularly
Retorsion (performative self-refutation)
A rights-granting behavioural sign will be forged, so caution favours the unforgeable mark
Biological substrate cannot currently be counterfeited to the standard required to fake suffering
A behavioural sign that is cheap to forge is unfit to serve as the criterion of moral standing
Prudence favours resting moral standing on currently-unforgeable biology rather than on forgeable behavioural signs
Any behavioural sign adopted as the public criterion for granting rights will be optimized against until it no longer tracks the underlying state
Criterion gaming (Goodhart's law / specification gaming)
The sorites paradox (the heap)
There is a precise grain-count at which a heap becomes a non-heap
Removing a single grain from a heap always leaves a heap
Pure storage is not a stable kind at mind-matching scale
Any physically realisable behaviour-matcher is an organised computer, not flat storage
The "simulated storm isn't wet" analogy conflates world-indices and the physical/phenomenal senses of wetness, so it cannot settle machine consciousness
Simulated rain is not wet in our world but is wet in the simulated world
Virtual realism (world-indexed properties of a simulation)
The emergence reply — experience can strongly emerge, so no need for fundamental mind
Strong emergence of experience from non-experiential matter is acceptable
The structure-and-dynamics argument — function omits the categorical/intrinsic
Phenomenal consciousness is categorical, not merely dispositional
The number of subjects in a system is graded by inter-part integration, not a discrete count
There are actual cases in which subject boundaries shift with the degree of integration
Swampman — a historyless duplicate would mean, so content cannot require selection history
A state's intentional content is fixed by the biological function of the mechanisms that produce and consume it
Only function-based content earns a principled distinction between meaning and mere triggering
The thinking animal in your chair is you, or there are two thinkers of your thoughts
A person persists just in case the same living human organism continues to exist
Trained mimicry is a rival explanation — next-token training makes distributional imitation of mind-talk the likelier account of the behavioural sign
Distributional imitation of human mind-talk, induced by next-token training, can produce an LLM's mind-like behavioural profile without realising the corresponding mental roles
Brown/Brownson — the person goes with the transplanted brain, so persistence tracks psychology, not the organism
Two-sided precaution — resting standing on unforgeable biology carries its own grave consequence, wrongful exclusion of a sentient mind
Precaution about sentience must weigh wrongful exclusion of a sentient mind alongside wrongful inclusion of a non-sentient one
The split brain shows divided access, not a divided subject — the flagship base case for merging fails
Split-brain patients exhibit divided perceptual access within a single undivided responding subject
Unforgeability is not flesh — the prudential argument favours unforgeability, not biology
What prudence favours is the hardest-to-forge sign, not biology specifically
Unforgeability is a proxy — hardness-to-fake is epistemic security against deception, not the value of tracking suffering
The unwarrantable premise — the zombie argument's conceivability premise cannot be cogently asserted
The zombie / conceivability argument against physicalism
No reasoner with a computable physical mind can warrantedly assert that the zombie scenario is ideally conceivable
The circularity objection to the LLM workspace argument presupposes a further fact beyond verbal poise, which the cerebral-celebrity account denies
Conscious contents are those that achieve widespread cerebral influence, especially over verbal report
A microphysical duplicate of a conscious being that wholly lacks phenomenal consciousness is conceivable
Access consciousness
Do large language models have moral status?
The AI hard problem is a model organism for the human hard problem
Would a consciousness-naive but introspecting LLM be able to fully explain its own self-perception from its mechanism?
All knowledge is fallible conjecture, resting on no certain foundations
At the moment of a free choice, more than one course of action is genuinely open to the agent
Unified experience is the obstruction to product structure, binding being the failure of consciousness to factorize into independent channels
Binding problem (unity of the percept)
An AI's blind-spot hard problem is structurally isomorphic to a conscious AI's hard problem
Consciousness is a higher-level feature caused by neurobiological processes
A state is conscious only if its content is globally broadcast to the brain's specialist systems
A mental state is conscious only if it is the object of a higher-order representation
Consciousness is identical to a system's integrated information (Φ)
Consciousness involves non-computable physical processes
The universe as a whole is the one fundamental conscious subject
A deflationist about phenomenal consciousness is committed, by parity of the mirrored dialectic, to compatibilism about free will
Every disanalogy between the two mirrored problem families traces to the thermodynamic arrow of time
Can we know that an external world exists, when every proof of it seems to presuppose it?
Folk-psychological mental states are posits of a false theory
Frankfurt cases dualize to the ganzfeld, freedom without alternatives mirroring consciousness without differentiation
Frankfurt cases (counterexamples to the principle of alternate possibilities)
Acting freely is compatible with the truth of determinism
Are mental states substrate-independent?
Persistence of an AI's explanatory gap under widening self-access cannot discriminate a structural blind spot from consciousness
Etiologies of hard-problem discourse in an AI (where the cause sits)
How and why does physical processing give rise to phenomenal experience?
What makes a person at one time the same person as a person at another time?
Personal identity is a simple, unanalyzable further fact, not constituted by physical or psychological continuity
Incentives can change an agent's behaviour only if genuine alternatives are open to the agent
How can a physical state be about anything?
Original intentionality is grounded in phenomenal consciousness
Phenomenal consciousness
All intentionality, original as well as derived, is relative to an interpretive stance
Is first-person acquaintance with one's own experience a genuine observation, or itself a representation?
Unobservability in principle (intersubjective vs. absolute)
Ablating a language model's J-space reduces experiential language in its self-reports
Justification consists in an infinite, non-repeating chain of reasons
Justification is the mutual support of beliefs within a system, not a linear chain from foundations
Large language models are an actual-world approximation of the perfect actor
The perfect actor (pain shown but never felt)
What, if anything, does Mary gain when she first sees red?
How can mental states have physical effects?
Each type of mental state is identical to a type of brain state
Phenomenal mental states are caused by physical processes but have no physical effects
There are no strict laws connecting mental kinds with physical kinds
Phenomenal character is identical to representational content
For phenomenal concepts, the two intensions coincide
Phenomenal consciousness as standardly conceived is an introspective illusion
Reality's fundamental constituents are neither mental nor physical
Reality is fundamentally mental
Reality has two irreducible aspects, the mental as well as the physical
Holding an agent morally responsible is justified by its effects on future conduct rather than by backward-looking desert
Responsibility judgments are in part deliverances of an evolved moral instinct rather than reason, so they need not be rationally consistent
Agents in a fine-grained physics simulation could feel the simulated properties (have sim-qualia)
A simulation instances a phenomenon when the phenomenon is constituted by formal/observer-relative relations, but only depicts it when the phenomenon is constituted by intrinsic causal powers
Some beliefs are justified non-inferentially rather than by derivation from other beliefs
Basic (non-inferential) belief
The mind is the form of a living body
A theory of consciousness must meet the minimal requirements to count as one
Minimal requirements for a theory of consciousness
Workspace-like functional organization is a solution that learning systems converge on under computational pressure
The workspace architecture of a language model is separable from any self-like point of view
The AI effect
Validity (logical)
Jo Cameron — lifelong pain and anxiety insensitivity from FAAH-OUT
The mind-body problem
Pain asymbolia (pain felt but not minded)
Philosophy of mind
Rendering test fixture (every book feature)
Sourcehood (ultimate origination)
Super-spartans (pain felt but never shown)
Transcendental argument
The Turing test (the imitation game)
Do agents ever act of their own free will?
